message:
fix(inet): bound user-supplied address-text reads in inet_abi (bd-z4k96 class)

REVIEW round 5: four ABI entry points dereferenced caller-supplied
*const c_char pointers via CStr::from_ptr without a length bound.
A non-NUL-terminated argument walked arbitrary process memory until
hitting an accidental zero — same info-leak / segfault class that
bd-z4k96 closed for setlocale/textdomain/bindtextdomain/newlocale,
that cab4670a closed for iconv_open, and that 6f1e3036 closed for
dlopen/dlsym strict-mode.

Affected sites:
  - inet_pton(af, src, dst)  L83  → src walked unbounded
  - inet_aton(cp, inp)        L181 → cp walked unbounded
  - inet_addr(cp)             L240 → cp walked unbounded
  - if_nametoindex(ifname)    L282 → ifname walked unbounded

Reachability: every program that parses an IP address from a
caller-controlled string (e.g. config-file readers, network-protocol
parsers, getaddrinfo fallbacks) reaches inet_pton/inet_aton/inet_addr.
if_nametoindex is reached by every routing/socket-options-by-iface
path. An attacker-supplied or memory-corrupted text pointer became
an arbitrary-read primitive across the libc.so boundary.

Fix: add the standard read_bounded_cstr helper (mirrors locale/iconv/
dlfcn copies — eventually candidate for a shared util) that wraps
scan_c_string + known_remaining and returns Option<Vec<u8>>; replace
each unbounded CStr::from_ptr on a user pointer with the bounded
read; on None, return the function's standard "invalid input"
sentinel (0/INADDR_NONE/-1 with errno).

Full abi suite: 3120/3120 pass across 76 suites including the
existing arpa/inet differential harness (7/7) and the inet_abi
integration suite (64/64).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
